financial.entity: The Siam Cement Public Company Limited (SCC.BK)
financial.note: SCC holds ~72% stake; SCGP is a key pillar of the SCG conglomerate, benefiting from group-wide procurement and credit synergy.
Indonesian subsidiary; its recovery toward net profit breakeven in 2H26 (following gas contract restructuring and price hikes) is a critical earnings catalyst.

supply_chain.note: RCP costs are a major margin driver; stable pricing in early 2026 has supported a recovery in gross margins (targeting 19.5% for 1Q26).
Packaging manufacturing is energy-intensive; Fajar's transition to a new energy mix in 2026 is expected to save THB 800–850m annually.
Vietnam and Indonesia are the primary growth engines; 2026 sales growth is driven by these high-margin consumer-linked packaging investments.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Secures long-term RCP supply and supports SCGP's circular economy/ESG rating (AAA).

competitors.note: Direct domestic rival in the industrial packaging paper segment.
Competes in the consumer packaging space (Glass/Cans vs. SCGP's Rigid/Flexible plastics).

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As a major exporter, SCGP's paper utilization (target 90% in 2026) is highly sensitive to China's industrial and consumption recovery.
A weaker Baht boosts THB-denominated revenue from overseas subsidiaries and enhances export competitiveness.
Directly correlates with the performance of the Integrated Packaging Chain (IPC) in ASEAN markets.
